title: genista cinerea subsp. leptoclada
intro: Genista cinerea subsp. leptoclada, also known as the Grey broom or Broom plant, is a shrubby legume plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Genista cinerea subsp. leptoclada has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, alleviating menstrual cramps, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or added to other herbal remedies.
